article.title: Repo of Knowledge
article.snippet: Fresh paint, building libraries, and starting side-projects (again). Welcome to new suzzy games website!

text.b: Welcome to the new and improved suzzy games website!

article.text: Absolutely everything on this site has been either upgraded or replaced entirely. The blog system went from janky to proper, pages are now full of content and trivia instead of being half-baked, and a new library was built. More on this later! I dedicated an entire weekend to turn this site from potato to french fry. I absolutely love how it looks now and I hope you do too.
article.quote: Sometimes I open up the website just to stare at it @_@

image.caption: New and Improved!
image.alt: A 4 by 4 grid showing the old site on top and the new site design on the bottom

article.text: Are you a developer? Then this chapter is for you! Applications and oAuth2 are now available for everybody! This allows third-party developers to safely access your profile and the game data that you decide to share with them.
New features and subsequent documentation on those features will become available for developers in the library. Which brings us to the next topic...

image.caption: oAuth2 User Interface (You've seen one of these before right?)
image.alt: An example oAuth2 prompt asking the user to allow access to certain account permissions

article.text: The library is a new section of the suzzy website! It contains legal stuff, branding guidelines, and most importantly documentation for developers. If you're really bored check out the branding article! It contains little bits of trivia on all past suzzy projects.

image.caption: Library Homepage (Its Boo!)
image.alt: A preview for the suzzy games library with the mascot sitting on a bean bag in their room reading a book

article.text: Introducing BECCA2D! An in-house 2D Game Engine designed from the ground up to be lightweight, efficient, extensible, and run in Google Chrome. sorry firefox users :p

I'm planning on using this engine in a new side-project, a side-scrolling style platformer fangame based on a certain fish vtuber I've taken a liking to recently. This fangame also shares a considerable amount of features with Project Monke, so this will also serve as a personal training ground of sorts.
But that's all I'm sharing about this side-project for today! I'll be making a dedicated post on the engine showcasing its features, design, and reasoning soon™
So that's my content dump for the month. Again I'm still working on new stuff and it'll all be done and released eventually gimme like six months or something :P

image.caption: ok that's it see you soon™
image.alt: A man holding the ball standing in front a white board with the words soon trademarked

article.quote: p.s. I got a drawing tablet! nothing crazy just a wacom one! expect more doodles all over the site now
p.p.s still no blog comments btw >.>
